title: Room Housekeeping
description: Standard for cleaning a room after checkout: sequence, timing, cleanliness standard.

steps.title: Preparation and entry
steps.content: Check the report: checkout clean or stayover clean (different standards). Knock 3 times with pauses, announce 'Housekeeping!'. Open with keycard, leave the door open (protection against theft accusations). Put on gloves.
steps.key_takeaway: Three knocks + 'housekeeping' + open door. This protects the staff.

steps.title: Inspection and trash removal
steps.content: Window — open for 5-10 minutes airing. Check for guest belongings left behind — immediately log in the lost-and-found register with a photo. Trash: three bins to the hallway — general, paper/plastic, hazardous (glass, blades).
steps.key_takeaway: Forgotten item = lost-and-found log + photo. No record = problem.

steps.title: Bed linen change
steps.content: Remove linen WITHOUT shaking (mites, insects). Visual mattress inspection — stains, damage. Make the bed to hotel standard: fitted sheet → duvet cover → pillows → bedspread. Towels — only folded 'fan-style' on the rack. Robes — on the bathroom door.
steps.key_takeaway: Never shake linen. Could spread mites to other rooms.

steps.title: Bathroom (the most critical)
steps.content: Order: toilet → sink → shower/tub → mirror → floor. Detergent acts for 3 minutes, don't wipe immediately. Limescale — vinegar or dedicated product, never abrasives. Hair in drains goes to trash, not flushed (clogs).
steps.key_takeaway: Toilet first, floor last. Reverse order = cross-contamination.

steps.title: Dust and surfaces
steps.content: Top-down: lamps and top of wardrobe first, then tables and nightstands, then baseboards. Remote, switches, handles — disinfect separately (90% of bacteria in the room). Phone — wipe handset and base. Glass — spray + lint-free cloth.
steps.key_takeaway: Top-down. Remote and handles are the top bacteria sources.

steps.title: Replenishing amenities
steps.content: Checklist: bottled water (2x), shampoo, gel, soap, shower cap, comb, hotel info folder, pen, notepad, trash liner. Check minibar against inventory — log what the guest used, submit to the supervisor for billing.
steps.key_takeaway: Minibar log on the card. Missed it = hotel loses money.

steps.title: Final inspection and handover
steps.content: Step 2 meters back from the door and inspect the room like a guest: everything in place, fresh smell, curtains per standard. Supervisor check: spot-check 1 in 4 rooms, signature on checklist. Only after signature is the room sellable in PMS.
steps.key_takeaway: Look like a guest, not like a cleaner. Two meters from the door is the right position.

quiz.question: What do you do before entering a room?
quiz.explanation: Three knocks is IHG/Marriott standard. Open door protects against accusations.

quiz.question: In what order do you clean the bathroom?
quiz.explanation: Toilet first, floor last. Reverse order spreads bacteria.

quiz.question: Where do you log guest belongings left behind?
quiz.explanation: Log + photo — mandatory. Legal protection for both hotel and staff.

quiz.question: What must you NOT do with linen?
quiz.explanation: Shaking can spread mites and insects to other rooms.

quiz.question: What requires separate disinfection?
quiz.explanation: Touchpoints carry 90% of bacteria. Floor is secondary.

manager_checklist: Housekeeper knocks 3 times and leaves door open
Performs final inspection 2 meters from the door

kpi.how_to_measure: Housekeeping management system or manual timing

kpi.metric: Rooms passing inspection on first try
